title: rosa coriifolia var. friesii
intro: Rosa coriifolia var. friesii, also known as the Himalayan rose or wild rose, is a plant species belonging to the family Rosaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rosa coriifolia var. friesii are numerous, and it is used to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the form of dried flowers, leaves, and roots, to extract its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to the scientific study, rosa coriifolia var. friesii, being part of the Rosa genus, is likely beneficial for its antioxidant and free-radical scavenger properties, which could be beneficial in skincare and nutrition.
